name: Meeting Preparation
description: Draft an agenda, background brief, and discussion prompts for an upcoming meeting. Works for internal working groups, stakeholder briefings, and governance forums.

inputs_required.name: meeting_purpose
inputs_required.label: Meeting purpose
inputs_required.placeholder: e.g. Quarterly governance review, stakeholder briefing on new regulations

inputs_required.name: attendees
inputs_required.label: Attendees
inputs_required.description: Who is attending — names, titles, or organisations
inputs_required.placeholder: e.g. Deputy Secretary (chair), EL2 Policy Lead, 3 external stakeholder reps

inputs_required.name: context
inputs_required.label: Context
inputs_required.description: Any relevant background — what's the history, why now, what's at stake
inputs_required.placeholder: e.g. First meeting after a major policy announcement; some attendees have concerns

inputs_required.name: key_documents
inputs_required.label: Background materials (optional)
inputs_required.description: Paste relevant external documents or summaries. External, publicly available documents only — do not paste OFFICIAL: Sensitive material into public AI tools.
inputs_required.placeholder: Paste relevant documents or summaries here...

steps.name: Context & Objectives
steps.description: Defines what the meeting needs to achieve, key issues, attendee dynamics, and any pre-meeting actions.
steps.user_action_after: Review the objectives — do they match what you actually need from this meeting? Adjust the attendee analysis based on your direct knowledge of these people. Note any pre-meeting actions that need to happen before you move on.

steps.name: Draft Agenda
steps.description: Produces a structured agenda ordered to achieve the objectives, with time allocations and item types.
steps.user_action_after: Check the time allocations against your experience of how long this group takes. Adjust the order if needed. Circulate to a colleague or the chair for sense-check before finalising.

steps.name: Background Brief
steps.description: Drafts a brief for attendees covering context, key issues, and what they need to come prepared to contribute. Optional for smaller internal meetings.
steps.user_action_after: Check all [VERIFY] flags before circulating. Have a senior officer review before it goes out — this represents your agency. Update any [INSERT: document name] placeholders with actual references.

steps.name: Discussion Prompts
steps.description: Generates opening questions, deepening prompts, and decision-forcing questions for each agenda item. For the chair or facilitator.
steps.user_action_after: Select the prompts that feel right for your group — you don't need all of them. Have the decision-forcing questions ready but use them with judgment. Good facilitation reads the room.

beginner.notes: Start with the context analysis and agenda — that's the core of meeting preparation. The background brief and discussion prompts add value for more complex meetings; add them once you're comfortable.

intermediate.notes: Run the full workflow for any meeting that involves external stakeholders or significant decisions. For routine internal meetings, Steps 1 and 2 are usually enough.

advanced.notes: For high-stakes meetings, share the draft agenda with a key attendee in advance and note their reaction. Their instinctive response to the agenda tells you a lot about how the meeting will go.

known_limitations: The model cannot know the history between specific attendees, recent dynamics, or interpersonal sensitivities.
Statistics and program details in the background brief may be inaccurate — verify all before circulating.
Facilitation prompts are generic — adapt to the specific group and communication styles of key attendees.
This workflow produces AI-assisted drafts. The chair or meeting organiser remains responsible for the quality of the meeting.
